commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz Vario Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Vario OM904 Injector Pump-Nozzle Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Vario OM904 Injector Pump-Nozzle Failure - Why Commercial Diesels Lose Power
problems.rootCause: The OM904 unit injector pump-nozzle system relies on precise electronic coding and high-pressure fuel delivery. Over time, internal wear, contaminated diesel, or incorrect calibration causes pressure drops, triggering fault codes 0 90 44 through 0 93 45 and severe rough running.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, recalibration of existing pump-nozzle units, ECU software reset, and live data verification.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if mechanical wear is absent; temporary fix for early coding or software drift errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of faulty cylinder injectors, installation of new Bosch-coded units, high-pressure rail testing, and system adaptation.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory performance if the engine block and turbo remain sound; addresses isolated or dual-cylinder failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a reconditioned long block with upgraded injection components, pre-coded injectors, bench-tested and ready for installation.
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term commercial solution; eliminates cumulative wear on the pump drive, injectors, and associated main bearings.

problems.recommendation: We recommend the intermediate pump-nozzle replacement only when a single cylinder fails and compression testing confirms healthy block integrity. For Mercedes Benz Vario units showing multi-cylinder injector faults alongside high mileage or oil dilution, a full rebuilt OM904 engine is significantly more cost-effective and prevents costly repeat breakdowns.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM904 Mercedes Benz Vario engine prices

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Vario OM602 Injector Pump Drive Gear Wear
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Vario OM602 Injector Pump Drive Gear Wear - Why Early Models Fail to Start
problems.rootCause: Pre-2006 OM602 engines utilise a composite injector pump drive gear that gradually strips under high torsion. Once the teeth shear, the fuel pump desynchronises from the crankshaft, causing immediate non-starts, zero fuel delivery, and potential pump vane scoring.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Drive Gear & Timing Chain Assembly
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front engine disassembly, replacement of stripped drive gear with upgraded metal variant, new timing chain tensioners, and static timing reset.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential preventative and repair step for early OM602 models; restores ignition sync but assumes fuel pump internals are undamaged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a reconditioned OM602 long block with factory-upgraded metal drive gear, recalibrated injection pump, and full gasket set.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent resolution for worn gears; highly recommended when pump vanes show scoring from timing slippage or if the chassis exceeds 150k miles.

problems.recommendation: If your Mercedes Benz Vario 512D or 812D cranks normally but will not fire, the drive gear is the primary suspect. We advise immediate replacement of the timing gear assembly; however, if diagnostic pressure tests confirm internal pump damage from desynchronisation, proceeding directly to a rebuilt OM602 unit saves extensive labour and guarantees long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM602 Mercedes Benz Vario engine replacement

problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Vario Turbocharger Failure - Black Smoke and Restricted Power Explained
problems.rootCause: Commercial towing and sustained high-load driving accelerate wear on the turbocharger’s centre bearing and variable geometry actuator linkage. Bearing collapse allows oil to escape into the intake manifold, triggering P0299 boost pressure fault codes and heavy exhaust smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal of failed turbo, installation of a remanufactured or new unit, intercooler flush, EGR cleaning, and new oil feed/return lines.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost pressure and fuel economy; viable only if the engine block oil galleries remain completely uncontaminated.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a reconditioned engine with upgraded turbocharger assembly, renewed oil supply system, and comprehensive cooling flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary when turbo failure causes catastrophic bearing wear, oil starvation, or hydraulic lock in the cylinder head.

problems.recommendation: On a Mercedes Benz Vario used for heavy commercial payloads, we strongly recommend replacing the turbo alongside the oil feed lines during any intermediate repair to prevent recurrence. If the engine has been operated in limp mode with visible oil ingestion for extended periods, a full rebuilt unit is the only safe commercial option to protect the bottom end.
problems.cta: Compare OM904 turbo replacement vs engine rebuild costs for your Mercedes Benz Vario

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Vario OM616 Head Gasket Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Vario OM616 Head Gasket Failure - Coolant Loss and White Exhaust Smoke
problems.rootCause: The early naturally aspirated OM616 operates under high static compression with a standard multi-layer steel gasket that degrades under severe thermal cycling. Micro-cracks in the cylinder head or deck warping allow coolant to leak into the combustion chamber, producing persistent white exhaust smoke and overheating.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Head Gasket & Cylinder Head Refurbishment
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Top-end engine removal, professional head resurfacing, installation of an upgraded reinforced MLS gasket, and cooling system pressure test.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for early-stage leaks; requires immediate attention to prevent hydrolock or crankshaft bearing corrosion from coolant ingress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully reconditioned block with upgraded head gasket, pressure-tested cylinder head, and renewed glow plug harness.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage early T1N models; eliminates underlying casting weaknesses and resolves concurrent electrical degradation.

problems.recommendation: For Mercedes Benz Vario operators maintaining early T1N chassis, a minor head gasket replacement offers strong value if the issue is caught before coolant contaminates the oil sump. Once oil-water mixing is confirmed, we advise upgrading to a complete rebuilt OM616 to avoid irreversible crankshaft and bearing failure.
problems.cta: Check rebuilt OM616 Mercedes Benz Vario engine availability and warranty terms

problems.group: Mercedes Benz Vario Euro 4/5 EGR and AdBlue System Faults
problems.h4: Mercedes Benz Vario Euro 4/5 EGR and AdBlue System Faults - Emissions Compliance Failures
problems.rootCause: Late-production Euro 4/5 variants integrate complex exhaust gas recirculation and AdBlue dosing systems to meet tightening emissions laws. Carbon accumulation in the EGR cooler and crystallisation in DEF injectors trigger fault code A0040940007, forcing the ECU into restricted power mode and failing emissions checks.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Valve Swap & DEF System Flush
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removal and replacement of stuck EGR valve, AdBlue injector cleaning or replacement, dosing pump recalibration, and ECU adaptation reset.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores full power output and MOT compliance; addresses surface valve sticking but does not resolve deep cooler blockage.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Euro 4/5 OM904 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a reconditioned OM904.964 with updated EGR hardware, recalibrated emissions mapping, and comprehensive DEF system diagnostics.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential when chronic exhaust backpressure causes piston ring glazing or turbo degradation; ensures long-term commercial compliance.

problems.recommendation: AdBlue and EGR faults on the Mercedes Benz Vario 818D are rarely isolated to a single component. We recommend a complete intermediate system clean and sensor recalibration first; however, if sustained backpressure has already compromised turbo efficiency or caused oil dilution, a rebuilt Euro 4/5 compliant unit is the only reliable commercial fix.
problems.cta: Get tailored quotes for Euro 4/5 Mercedes Benz Vario engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Benz Vario
finalCta.paragraph: When Mercedes Benz Vario engine problems disrupt your commercial operations or fleet schedules, downtime costs quickly outweigh initial repair expenses. Our marketplace connects you directly with vetted UK engine specialists who stock pre-coded rebuilt OM904, OM602, and OM616 units, all featuring upgraded components to prevent repeat factory failures. With nationwide delivery, transparent supply & fit options, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on every engine, you can bypass main dealer markups and keep your commercial vehicles running profitably.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Benz Vario Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Benz Vario and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz Vario Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Benz Vario is listed below with compatible engine codes, production years, and documented failure notes to ensure accurate replacement matching.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Vario Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vario 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest and earliest naturally aspirated unit in the Vario lineup, this 2.4L diesel powered initial T1N chassis from launch until 2004. Built for straightforward reliability and ease of maintenance, it served as the entry-level workhorse for light commercial applications before being phased out in favour of more efficient forced-induction alternatives.
commonFailurePoints: Multi-layer steel head gasket degradation under thermal cycling
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vario 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vario 2.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: Bridging the gap between early naturally aspirated units and modern common-rail systems, this 2.9L five-cylinder diesel offered improved torque delivery for heavier commercial payloads. It remained a staple in the mid-range Vario variants throughout the late 1990s and 2000s, praised for its robust bottom end despite known fuel delivery vulnerabilities.
commonFailurePoints: Composite injector pump drive gear shear causing timing desynchronisation
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vario 2.9L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vario 4.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Forming the backbone of the Vario commercial range, the 4.2L turbocharged OM904 family introduced high-pressure unit injection and advanced engine management. Spanning multiple power outputs from early Euro 2 configurations to late-production Euro 5 AdBlue-equipped variants, it remains the most searched displacement for fleet replacements.
commonFailurePoints: Unit injector pump-nozzle failure triggering fault codes 0 90 44 to 0 93 45
with EGR valve sticking on later Euro 4/5 models
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vario 4.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Vario 4.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: Developed as a higher-output variant of the OM904 architecture, the 4.3L turbo diesel delivered increased torque for demanding commercial towing and heavy chassis applications. Fitted to mid-generation 600 and 800 series models, it balances payload capacity with refined turbo management, though sustained high-load operation often accelerates induction component wear.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger centre bearing collapse causing boost pressure loss
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Vario 4.3L diesel engines

Pre-2006 OM602 injector pump drive gear shear causing non-start conditions
Euro 4/5 OM904.964 EGR valve sticking and AdBlue system fault A0040940007

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz Vario Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Covering the complete T1N production timeline from 1996 to 2013, this section helps Mercedes Benz Vario owners quickly identify their engine by model year and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK commercial vehicle specialists.

years.preview: T1N Platform Launch & Early Commercial Diesels
years.description: Launch year introduced the dedicated T1N commercial chassis, pairing heavy-duty suspension with naturally aspirated and early turbo diesel powertrains.
keyChanges: T1N platform replaces original T1 van architecture with strengthened ladder-frame chassis
OM616 2.4L and OM602 2.9L five-cylinder diesels introduced for lighter 500/600 series
OM904 4.2L turbo added for heavier 800 series payloads and crew cab variants
Manual gearbox focus with basic mechanical engine management and no emissions after-treatment
knownFor: Exceptional mechanical simplicity makes early OM602 units highly sought for rebuilds
OM616 naturally aspirated engines suffer cylinder head gasket fatigue under sustained thermal load
Injection pump drive gear wear on pre-2004 OM602 models causes sudden non-start conditions
Glow plug harness degradation leads to persistent cold-start hesitation on older T1N chassis
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996 Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engines
years.ticker: 512D engine replacement • OM602 injector pump drive gear wear • 814D engine swap cost
enquiries: OM602 injector pump drive gear wear

years.description: Mid-cycle emissions compliance triggered a significant powertrain update, introducing the higher-torque 4.3L unit for mid-weight chassis.
keyChanges: Euro 3 emissions standards mandate updated engine mapping and revised EGR hardware
OM904.923 4.3L turbo diesel launched to bridge power gap between 4.2L and heavy haulers
OM602 2.9L begins phased retirement across lighter 500/600 series variants
Updated cooling system capacity and reinforced turbo mounting points address early commercial fatigue
knownFor: 4.3L displacement delivers noticeable torque improvement for minibus conversions and heavy payload duties
Turbocharger centre bearing wear emerges as the dominant high-mileage failure on sustained commercial routes
EGR cooler blockage triggers performance limp mode and requires specialist cleaning or replacement
Pre-2006 OM602 non-start faults peak as ageing drive gear teeth finally shear under load
years.cta: Compare 2004 Mercedes Benz Vario engine prices
years.ticker: 815D engine replacement • OM904 turbo failure symptoms • 4.3L turbo diesel Vario cost

years.description: Euro 4 regulations forced the introduction of selective catalytic reduction, creating the highest-output and most complex Vario powertrain to date.
keyChanges: AdBlue dosing system and SCR catalysts fitted to flagship 618D and 818D models
OM904.964 4.2L turbo launched with reinforced internals to meet 177bhp Euro 4 targets
Revised cylinder head casting and upgraded high-pressure fuel rail systems implemented
Automatic transmission options expanded for fleet operators requiring reduced driver fatigue
knownFor: Highest power output in Vario history enables reliable modern motorway speeds with heavy loads
Unit injector pump-nozzle failure (codes 0 90 44 to 0 93 45) peaks across commercial fleets
AdBlue crystallisation and EGR valve sticking trigger fault A0040940007 requiring specialist diagnostics
Late-model Euro 4 engines command premium replacement prices due to integrated emissions hardware
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006 Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engines
years.ticker: 818D engine replacement • AdBlue system fault A0040940007 • OM904 pump-nozzle coding service

years.description: The final phase refined the OM904 family for Euro 5 standards, prioritising reliability and fleet longevity before commercial production concluded.
keyChanges: Euro 5 emissions updates tighten particulate filters and NOx reduction parameters
Minor ECU software revisions optimise AdBlue dosing intervals and DEF consumption rates
Facelifted interior trim and updated commercial body options roll out across 800 series
Production officially concludes in 2013 as Mercedes shifts heavy commercial focus exclusively to Sprinter
knownFor: Late-model chassis represent the peak of Vario engineering and long-term commercial reliability
Strict DPF regeneration cycles and AdBlue maintenance increase dependence on specialist diagnostic tools
High residual values persist for verified minibus conversions with documented service histories
Core exchange demand rises as final-year units reach commercial replacement mileage thresholds
years.cta: Enquire about 2013 Mercedes Benz Vario engine availability
years.ticker: 2013 Vario engine cost • Euro 5 replacement compatibility • 818D AdBlue bypass risks

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz Vario Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mercedes Benz Vario engine?
items.answer: A Mercedes Benz Vario engine replacement typically costs £800-£2,500 for used or reconditioned units supply-only, with OM904LA engines at the higher end of that range. Supply & fit installation adds £400-£800 depending on chassis type, workshop location and emissions hardware complexity. Final prices vary by engine code, variant and whether pump-nozzle recalibration is included.
keyPoints: Used OM602 2.9L engines start around £800-£1,200 supply-only across UK breakers
Reconditioned OM904LA with injection pump recalibration typically £1,400-£2,500 supply-only
Euro 4/5 OM904.964 units command premium pricing due to integrated AdBlue hardware
Supply & fit labour typically adds £400-£800 depending on chassis access requirements
items.cta: Get tailored quotes for Mercedes Benz Vario engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: What are the most common Mercedes Benz Vario 814D engine problems?
items.answer: The Mercedes Benz Vario 814D's OM904 engine most commonly suffers injector pump-nozzle failure triggering fault codes 0 90 44 through 0 93 45. Turbocharger bearing wear causing loss of power and black smoke is the second most reported issue. Both problems often drive complete engine replacement searches among commercial fleet operators.
keyPoints: OM904 pump-nozzle unit failure requires Bosch coding and specialist diagnostic recalibration
Turbocharger centre bearing collapse triggers P0299 fault code and severe power loss
EGR valve sticking on Euro 3/4 models causes limp mode and emissions faults
Crankshaft position sensor failure (code 1 03 08) prevents engine starting entirely
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM904 Mercedes Benz Vario engine prices with pump-nozzle recalibration included

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes Benz Vario engine or buying a new vehicle?
items.answer: Replacing a Mercedes Benz Vario engine is usually worth it if the chassis, gearbox and body remain structurally sound. A reconditioned OM904 or OM602 unit costs £1,400-£2,500 supply-only versus £25,000+ for a comparable replacement commercial vehicle. For fleet operators, engine replacement minimises operational downtime and preserves existing body conversions or minibus passenger fitouts.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engine supply & fit typically costs 5-10% of new vehicle purchase price
Existing minibus, crew cab or specialist body conversions retain significant residual value
OM904 and OM602 rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing known failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty on replacement engines protects against early repeat failures
items.cta: Find out if your Mercedes Benz Vario is suitable for engine replacement

items.question: Which Mercedes Benz Vario engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The Mercedes Benz Vario OM602 2.9L five-cylinder diesel is widely considered the most reliable engine, particularly pre-2006 units with mechanical injection. The OM904LA 4.2L turbo is robust when injector pump-nozzle units are recalibrated at service intervals. Early OM616 2.4L naturally aspirated engines are simple but prone to head gasket fatigue under sustained commercial loads.
keyPoints: OM602 2.9L five-cylinder offers proven longevity with straightforward mechanical fuel delivery
OM904LA 4.2L turbo requires regular injector coding to prevent pump-nozzle cylinder failure
OM616 2.4L benefits from upgraded MLS head gasket during any reconditioning process
All Vario diesel engines use timing chains, not belts, reducing scheduled maintenance costs
items.cta: Compare reliable OM602 and OM904 Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engine options

items.question: How do I know if my Mercedes Benz Vario has an OM904, OM602 or OM616 engine?
items.answer: Enter your Mercedes Benz Vario registration number above to instantly identify your exact engine code via the official DVLA database. Alternatively, locate the engine data plate on the cylinder head or block: OM904 powers 814D/815D/818D variants, OM602 fits 512D/612D/812D models, and OM616 appears only in early T1N chassis from 1996-2004.
keyPoints: OM904LA 4.2L turbo engines power 814D, 815D, 816D and flagship 818D variants
OM602 2.9L five-cylinder units fit lighter 512D, 612D and 812D chassis models
OM616 2.4L naturally aspirated engines appear only in earliest 1996-2004 T1N production
Engine code stampings are visible on cylinder head or injection pump housing
items.cta: Enter your registration to identify your Mercedes Benz Vario engine code instantly

items.question: What causes the OM904 injector pump failure in Mercedes Benz Vario engines?
items.answer: OM904 injector pump-nozzle failure in Mercedes Benz Vario engines stems from internal wear in the unit injector assembly, triggering fault codes 0 90 44 through 0 93 45. Contaminated diesel fuel, infrequent servicing and high commercial mileage accelerate wear. Failed units cause rough idle, loss of power and excessive black smoke from the exhaust.
keyPoints: Unit injector pump-nozzle wear prevents precise fuel metering and cylinder combustion
Fault codes 0 90 44 to 0 93 45 identify which cylinder's injector has failed
Contaminated diesel or water ingress accelerates internal pump-nozzle component degradation
Recalibration requires Bosch-certified equipment and specialist diagnostic software access
items.cta: Get quotes for OM904 Mercedes Benz Vario engines with pump-nozzle recalibration included

items.question: Can I fit a later Euro 4/5 OM904.964 engine into my earlier Mercedes Benz Vario?
items.answer: Fitting a Euro 4/5 OM904.964 engine into an earlier Mercedes Benz Vario is technically possible but requires ECU remapping, AdBlue system integration and emissions hardware compatibility checks. Most commercial operators find it more cost-effective to replace with a like-for-like Euro 2 or Euro 3 OM904 unit that matches existing wiring looms and exhaust configurations.
keyPoints: Euro 4/5 OM904.964 engines include AdBlue dosing hardware not present on earlier chassis
ECU software, wiring harness and exhaust routing differ significantly between emissions standards
Like-for-like engine replacement avoids complex retrofitting and potential MOT emissions test failures
Specialist commercial vehicle workshops can advise on cross-compatibility before purchase commitment
items.cta: Compare compatible OM904 Mercedes Benz Vario engines matched to your chassis emissions standard

items.question: How long should a Mercedes Benz Vario diesel engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With proper commercial maintenance, Mercedes Benz Vario diesel engines typically achieve 200,000-250,000 miles before requiring major overhaul or replacement. OM602 2.9L units often exceed 250,000 miles with regular injector pump servicing. OM904LA 4.2L turbos reach similar mileage when pump-nozzle units are recalibrated and turbo oil feed lines are replaced preventatively at 120,000-mile intervals.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes with commercial-grade specification lubricants extend Vario engine lifespan significantly
OM904 injector pump-nozzle recalibration every 80,000-100,000 miles prevents catastrophic cylinder failure
Turbocharger oil feed line replacement at 120,000 miles prevents bearing collapse and oil starvation
Glow plug harness inspection and replacement prevents cold-start electrical issues on OM616 engines
items.cta: Find reconditioned Mercedes Benz Vario engines with upgraded components for extended longevity

items.question: Do Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engines come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from vetted UK commercial vehicle specialists. Reconditioned OM904 and OM602 units typically carry 12-24 month coverage depending on the supplier and whether pump-nozzle recalibration or turbo upgrades are included in the rebuild specification. Always confirm exact warranty terms, exclusions and claim procedures before final purchase commitment.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty applies to all EngineMarket network replacement engines
Reconditioned units with pump-nozzle recalibration often qualify for extended 24-month coverage options
Warranty typically covers internal mechanical components but excludes external accessories and installation labour
Keep full service records and installation receipts to validate any future warranty claims
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Vario replacement engines with 12-month warranty from UK specialists

items.question: What's the difference between used, reconditioned and remanufactured Mercedes Benz Vario engines?
items.answer: Used Mercedes Benz Vario engines are low-mileage donor units sold as-is with limited testing. Reconditioned engines receive stripped rebuilds with new gaskets, seals and pump-nozzle recalibration for OM904 units. Remanufactured engines are built to original factory specifications with new pistons, bearings and dyno testing, offering the highest reliability but at a premium price point for commercial fleet applications.
keyPoints: Used engines offer lowest cost but carry unknown injection pump and turbo condition risks
Reconditioned OM904 units include injection pump-nozzle recalibration and upgraded gasket sets
Remanufactured long blocks feature new internal components and full dyno performance validation
All EngineMarket options include minimum 12-month warranty regardless of condition grade
items.cta: Compare used, reconditioned and remanufactured Mercedes Benz Vario engine options with warranty
